children.code:
def ComputeNewStock(validated_items: list, product_stock_map: dict) -> list:
    stock_updates = []
    for item in validated_items:
        product_id = item['product_id']
        quantity = item['quantity']
        new_stock = product_stock_map[product_id] - quantity
        stock_updates.append({'product_id': product_id, 'new_stock': new_stock})
    return stock_updates

postconditions: If success, order status changed to completed

CancelOrder.purpose: Cancel an order: validate order status is pending or paid, restore stock, refund balance if paid, update status to cancelled.
CancelOrder.behavior: Get order via orders.get, verify status is pending or paid. For each item, restore stock via products.update. If status was paid, refund balance to user via users.update. Update order status to cancelled via orders.update. Return success or error.
postconditions: If success, order status cancelled, stock restored, balance refunded if paid

ListOrders.purpose: List orders with optional filters by user and status.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success status, message, and data (list of orders).

ListOrders.behavior: Extract user_filter and status_filter from order_data. Call orders.list with those filters. Return success with list of orders.
postconditions: Returns list of orders matching filters

GetUserOrders.purpose: Get all orders for a specific user, calculate total spent and count per status.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success status, message, and data with orders, total_spent, status_counts.

GetUserOrders.behavior: Extract user_id from order_data. Call orders.list with user_id filter. Compute total_spent by summing total_price of all orders. Compute status_counts by counting orders per status. Return success with orders list, total_spent, and status_counts.
postconditions: Returns user orders with aggregated statistics

ListProducts.purpose: List products with optional low stock filter.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success status, message, and data (list of products).

ListProducts.behavior: Extract low_stock from order_data (default False). Call products.list with low_stock flag. Return success with list of products.
postconditions: Returns list of products

decomposition_rationale: The parent function manage_order dispatches based on command. Each command is handled by a dedicated child function: CreateOrder, PayOrder, ShipOrder, CompleteOrder, CancelOrder, ListOrders, GetUserOrders, ListProducts. These children directly implement the functional requirements. The parent coordinates by parsing the command and calling the appropriate child, passing order_data. Each child accesses the necessary data sources via requested_capabilities. The parent does not directly access data sources; children do. The global_vars are distributed among children according to their data operations.

subprd.purpose: Order Management System
subprd.description:
INPUT FORMAT:
  Format: json
  Description: Input is a JSON object with command and order_data fields. The function manage_order(command, order_data) is called.
  Schema:
    command: string (one of: create_order, pay_order, ship_order, complete_order, cancel_order, list_orders, get_user_orders, list_products)
    order_data: object containing relevant fields: user_id (int), order_id (int), items (list of {product_id, quantity}), status_filter (string), user_filter (int), low_stock (bool)
  Examples:
    {"command": "create_order", "order_data": {"user_id": 1, "items": [{"product_id": 2, "quantity": 2}]}}
    {"command": "pay_order", "order_data": {"order_id": 1}}
    {"command": "list_products", "order_data": {"low_stock": true}}

OUTPUT FORMAT:
  Format: json
  Description: Output is a JSON object with success, message, and data fields.
  Schema:
    success: boolean
    message: string
    data: object (varies by command)
  Examples:
    {"success": true, "message": "\u8ba2\u5355\u521b\u5efa\u6210\u529f", "data": {"order_id": 1, "total_price": 199.8}}
    {"success": false, "message": "\u5546\u54c1\u5e93\u5b58\u4e0d\u8db3", "data": {"product_id": 1, "available": 10, "requested": 15}}

Functional Requirements:
[FR-001] Create Order: User creates an order with user ID and item list. Check user existence, product stock, calculate total price, deduct stock, create order record with status pending.
[FR-002] Pay Order: User pays for an order. Check order exists and status is pending, check user balance, deduct balance, update order status to paid.
[FR-003] Ship Order: Ship an order. Check order exists and status is paid, update status to shipped.
[FR-004] Complete Order: Complete an order. Check order exists and status is shipped, update status to completed.
[FR-005] Cancel Order: Cancel an order. Check order exists and status is pending or paid, restore stock, refund balance if paid, update status to cancelled.
[FR-006] List Orders: List orders with optional filters by user and status. Display order details including items, total price, status, and time.
[FR-007] Get User Orders: Get all orders for a specific user. Calculate total spent and count of orders per status.
[FR-008] List Products: List products with info and stock. Optionally filter low stock products.

constraints.description: Storage: memory - Three independent in-memory data sources: users, products, orders.

acceptance_criteria.description: Create order with valid user and sufficient stock succeeds, stock deducted, order created with pending status.
Create order with insufficient stock returns error, no stock deduction.
Pay order with sufficient balance succeeds, balance deducted, status changed to paid.
Pay order with insufficient balance returns error, no balance deduction.
Cancel pending order restores stock, no refund, status cancelled.
Cancel paid order restores stock and refunds balance, status cancelled.
Order status transitions correctly: pending -> paid -> shipped -> completed.

CreateOrder.purpose: Create a new order: validate user and products, calculate total, deduct stock, create order record.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success status, message, and data (order_id, total_price).

CreateOrder.behavior: Check user exists via users.get. For each item, check product exists and stock sufficient via products.get. Calculate total price. Deduct stock via products.update. Create order via orders.create. Return success with order_id and total_price, or error with details.
postconditions: If success, order created with status pending, stock deducted

PayOrder.purpose: Process payment for an order: validate order status, check user balance, deduct balance, update order status to paid.
PayOrder.behavior: Get order via orders.get, verify status is pending. Get user via users.get, check balance >= total_price. Deduct balance via users.update. Update order status to paid via orders.update. Return success or error.
postconditions: If success, order status changed to paid, user balance deducted

ShipOrder.purpose: Ship an order: validate order status is paid, update status to shipped.
ShipOrder.behavior: Get order via orders.get, verify status is paid. Update order status to shipped via orders.update. Return success or error.
postconditions: If success, order status changed to shipped

CompleteOrder.purpose: Complete an order: validate order status is shipped, update status to completed.
CompleteOrder.behavior: Get order via orders.get, verify status is shipped. Update order status to completed via orders.update. Return success or error.
postconditions: If success, order status changed to completed

CancelOrder.purpose: Cancel an order: validate order status is pending or paid, restore stock, refund balance if paid, update status to cancelled.
CancelOrder.behavior: Get order via orders.get, verify status is pending or paid. For each item, restore stock via products.update. If status was paid, refund balance to user via users.update. Update order status to cancelled via orders.update. Return success or error.
postconditions: If success, order status cancelled, stock restored, balance refunded if paid
